2605.04595.title: A Queueing-Theoretic Framework for Stability Analysis of LLM Inference with KV Cache Memory Constraints
2605.04595.paper_summary: The rapid adoption of large language models (LLMs) has created significant challenges for efficient inference at scale. Unlike traditional workloads, LLM inference is constrained by both computation and the memory overhead of key-value (KV) caching, which accelerates decoding but quickly exhausts GPU memory. In this paper, we introduce the first queueing-theoretic framework that explicitly incorporates both computation and GPU memory constraints into the analysis of LLM inference. Based on this framework, we derive rigorous stability and instability conditions that determine whether an LLM inference service can sustain incoming demand without unbounded queue growth. This result offers a powerful tool for system deployment, potentially addressing the core challenge of GPU provisioning. By combining an estimated request arrival rate with our derived stable service rate, operators can calculate the necessary cluster size to avoid both costly over-purchasing and performance-violating under-provisioning. We further validate our theoretical predictions through extensive experiments in real GPU production environments. Our results show that the predicted stability conditions are highly accurate, with deviations typically within 10%.
2605.04595.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的快速採用為高效推理帶來了重大挑戰。與傳統工作負載不同，LLM 推理受到計算和關鍵值（KV）快取的記憶體開銷的限制，這雖然加速了解碼，但卻迅速耗盡 GPU 記憶體。在本文中，我們介紹了第一個排隊理論框架，明確將計算和 GPU 記憶體限制納入 LLM 推理分析中。基於這個框架，我們推導出嚴格的穩定性和不穩定性條件，以確定 LLM 推理服務是否能在不產生無限排隊增長的情況下維持進來的需求。這一結果為系統部署提供了一個強大的工具，可能解決 GPU 配置的核心挑戰。通過將估計的請求到達率與我們推導的穩定服務率相結合，運營商可以計算出必要的集群規模，以避免昂貴的過度購買和性能違規的不足配置。我們還通過在實際 GPU 生產環境中的廣泛實驗來驗證我們的理論預測。我們的結果顯示，預測的穩定性條件非常準確，偏差通常在 10% 以內。
2605.04595.author: Chengyi Nie et.al.
2605.04595.authors: Chengyi Nie, Nian Si, Zijie Zhou
